title: Developmental Immunology
authors: Benjamin JT, Molloy EJ, Maheshwari A / Fanaroff & Martin 2025

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Developmental Immunology?
ระบบ immune ของ neonatal ไม่ใช่ 'miniature adult' — มันคือ transient immunodeficiency + paradoxical hyperinflammation ที่ coexist พร้อมกัน. (1) Preterm infant มี neutrophil storage pool (NSP) เล็กมาก → sepsis = rapid depletion → neutropenia = poor prognosis; (2) Th2 skewing ใน neonatal T cells (epigenetic: hypomethylated CNS1) → impaired Th1 → ↑ vulnerability ต่อ intracellular pathogens; (3) Complement classic pathway แทบไม่มีประโยชน์แรกเกิด (ไม่มี specific IgG) → lectin/alternative pathway คือ first line; (4) TLR4 → NEC + sepsis; NLRP3 inflammasome → BPD + brain injury; unregulated cytokines → multiorgan damage; (5) Physiologic hypogammaglobulinemia nadir ที่ 3–4 เดือน → ↑ infection risk ใน ex-preterm. ความเข้าใจ system นี้ขับเคลื่อน decision ทุกอย่างตั้งแต่ sepsis workup ถึง vaccine timing.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย ontogeny ของ hematopoiesis: yolk sac → liver → bone marrow พร้อม timing ของแต่ละ site
อธิบาย neutrophil kinetics ใน neonatal period: NPP, NSP, release mechanism, และ reference ranges (Schmutz) ตาม gestational age
ระบุ defects ของ neonatal PMN: adhesion (selectin, CD11b/CD18), chemotaxis (timing recovery ตาม GA), phagocytosis (CD16/CD32 deficiency), respiratory burst, NET formation
อธิบาย complement system: classic, alternative, lectin pathways; complement levels ใน neonates (% adult); clinical significance ของ deficiency
อธิบาย TLR biology: TLR1–9 ligands + disease states; TLR4 กับ NEC/sepsis; CD14 + MyD88 signaling pathway
อธิบาย cytokine network ใน neonatal sepsis: TNF cascade, IL-1 superfamily + NLRP3 inflammasome, IL-6 (biomarker), IL-10 (anti-inflammatory), chemokines
อธิบาย T cell development ใน thymus; Th1/Th2/Th17/Treg subtypes; เหตุผล epigenetic ของ Th2 skewing ใน neonates
อธิบาย B cell development และ immunoglobulin classes (IgG/IgA/IgM/IgD/IgE): properties, half-life, placental transfer, complement activation
อธิบาย passive immunity: placental IgG transport (Fc receptor, timing), physiologic hypogammaglobulinemia, breast milk immunology (sIgA, lactoferrin, collectins)
ระบุ clinical implications: สิ่งที่บอก immune status (I/T ratio, ANC, CRP, IL-6, CD64), เมื่อใดพิจารณา IVIG/G-CSF, vaccine timing ใน ex-preterm
1. Hematopoiesis — Ontogeny
Yolk sac: week 3–10; first site of hematopoiesis; mainly erythropoiesis
Fetal liver: week 5–20–24; predominantly erythropoietic; myeloid progenitors abundant แต่ mature neutrophils ไม่พบ
Bone marrow: week 11 เริ่ม committed granulocytic progenitors ใน clavicular marrow; week 20 กลายเป็น major site; ดำเนินต่อตลอดชีวิต
CFU nomenclature: CFU-MIX → ผสม; CFU-GEMM → granulocyte + erythrocyte + monocyte + megakaryocyte; CFU-G → neutrophil only; CFU-GM → neutrophil + monocyte
Pluripotent stem cell → committed progenitors: driven by growth factors (G-CSF, GM-CSF, IL-3) from fibroblasts, endothelial cells, macrophages in hematopoietic microenvironment
2. Innate Immunity — Neutrophil (PMN) System
2.1 Kinetics of Production + Normal Values
PMN compartments: neutrophil proliferating pool (NPP) ~2×10⁹/kg; neutrophil storage pool (NSP) ~6×10⁹/kg → รวมกัน 90% ของ neutrophils ทั้งหมด
Preterm problem: NSP เล็กมาก ใน mid-gestation/preterm → rapidly exhausted ใน sepsis; NPP ก็เล็กกว่า adult ~10× (per kg BW) → limited compensatory capacity
Circulation time: circulate 6–8 hours → tissue 24 hours additional → die in tissue (ไม่กลับ vascular)
ANC peaks ที่ 12–24 hours หลังเกิด (term: 95th CI = 7800–14,500/µL) → nadir by 72h (~1750/µL) → stabilize 6.6 days

Schmutz Reference Ranges สำหรับ ANC (30,354 CBCs, 23–42 wk GA)
72–240 hours after birth: (5th–95th percentile) — >36 weeks: 2700–13,000/µL; 28–36 weeks: 1000–12,500/µL; <28 weeks: 1300–15,300/µL. ข้อดีของ study นี้: automated blood counting → consistent identification; large sample size ครอบคลุมทุก GA. Neutropenia = <1500/mm³ → strong suspicion infection (แต่ก็ดู preeclampsia, IVH, hemolytic disease). Persistent neutropenia ใน sepsis → ↑ risk overwhelming infection + mortality ใน NEC.
2.2 Left Shift + I/T Ratio
Left shift: ↑ immature neutrophils (band, metamyelocyte, myelocyte) ใน sepsis. I/T ratio = (bands + metamyelocytes + myelocytes) / total neutrophils
Band cells = sepsis marker; immature myelocytes + metamyelocytes = poor prognosis / may predict mortality (Mare et al Crit Care 2015)
ปัญหา: ไม่มี consensus บน surface markers สำหรับ immature vs mature neutrophils → inter-lab variability สูง (McKenna et al Front Immunol 2021)
2.3 Adhesion + Rolling Defects
Rolling: L-selectin (neutrophil) + addressins (GlyCAM-1, CD34, MAdCAM-1) บน endothelium; P-selectin + E-selectin บน inflamed endothelium + PSGL-1 บน neutrophil
Attachment: β₂-integrins: CD11a/CD18 (LFA-1), CD11b/CD18 (Mac-1) + CD11c/CD18 — จับ ICAM-1/ICAM-2 บน endothelium

Leukocyte Adhesion Deficiency (LAD) — Clinical Pearl
CD18 deficiency: hereditary absence ของ β₂-integrin → recurrent life-threatening infections; characteristic: delayed separation of umbilical cord (>21 days) + failure to accumulate neutrophils at infection sites + neutrophilia (neutrophils can't migrate out). มี LAD type I (CD18), type II (fucose transporter). ถ้าพบ neonate: cord >3 wks + no pus + neutrophilia → คิดถึง LAD ก่อน.
Neonatal defects: ↓ L-selectin expression + impaired shedding; ↓ CD11b/CD18; ↓ upregulation Mac-1 after bacterial stimulation (cord blood) ← แต่ postnatal samples ดีกว่ามาก
G-CSF: ↑ β₂-integrin แต่ ↓ L-selectin; dexamethasone: ↓ β₂-integrin expression → ↓ activation/hyperinflammation
2.4 Chemotaxis Defects
Chemotaxis = directed migration ตาม gradient ของ chemoattractants (f-MLP, IL-8/CXC, C5a, LTB4)
Term neonates: achieve normal chemotaxis by 2 weeks after birth
Preterm 34–36 wk: normal chemotaxis by 40–42 weeks PCA
Preterm <34 wk: chemotaxis ยังคง impaired เทียบกับ adult แม้ที่ 42 wk PCA
Mechanism: ↑ poorly-motile neutrophil subpopulation; impaired calcium mobilization; aberrant NF-κB; ↓ Mac-1 → impaired matrix interaction
Drug effects: MgSO₄ (intrapartum): ↓ chemotaxis + random motility; theophylline high range (≥15 µg/mL): ↓ chemotaxis; indomethacin: ↓ chemotaxis (more in preterm); G-CSF/GM-CSF: ↑ chemotaxis
2.5 Phagocytosis Defects
Opsonins required: IgG (Fc receptor: CD16/FcRIII, CD32/FcRII, CD64/FcRI), C3b (CR1), iC3b (CR3/Mac-1)

Preterm defects: ↓ CD16 + ↓ CD32; CD16 normalizes in 3 weeks; CD32 deficiency does NOT correct with time — CD32 = high-affinity receptor for IgG2 (against encapsulated bacteria) → persistent vulnerability ต่อ encapsulated bacteria
CD64: ↑ ใน neonates (preterm + term) vs adults; ไม่ affected by respiratory distress; emerging role as early marker for bacterial infection

IVIG ใน Neonatal Sepsis — INIS Trial (NEJM 2011)
INIS trial (n=2,416, NEJM 2011;365:1201): IVIG as adjunct ใน suspected/proven neonatal infection → NO improvement in mortality or neurodevelopmental outcome at 2 years. Limitation: current IVIG formulations may lack adequate concentrations ของ antibodies ต่อ neonatal pathogens. IVIG ไม่แนะนำ routine สำหรับ neonatal sepsis. G-CSF/GM-CSF ก็ไม่แนะนำ routine use (เพิ่ม neutrophil count แต่ไม่ improve mortality; PROGRAMS study: GM-CSF prophylaxis → no sepsis reduction + worse respiratory outcomes at 2 and 5 years).
2.6 Microbicidal Activity + NET Formation
Respiratory burst: NADPH oxidase → O₂•⁻ → H₂O₂ → •OH. Myeloperoxidase (MPO)-H₂O₂-halide system เพิ่ม bactericidal efficacy ~50×
Preterm: ↑ O₂ consumption แต่ overall respiratory burst depressed; ↓ intracellular killing S. aureus/E. coli ใน preterm (term = normal)
Nonoxidative mechanisms: defensins, BPI (binds/blocks LPS), LL-37, lysozyme, lactoferrin (iron chelation), azuricidin, cathelicidins

NETs (Neutrophil Extracellular Traps): chromatin + histones + MPO released from dying neutrophils → trap + kill microbes extracellularly. Neonatal neutrophils: markedly deficient ใน NET formation (Yost et al Blood 2009). NETs implicated ใน NEC pathogenesis (Vincent Sci Rep 2018)
Granules: azurophilic (MPO, defensins, BPI) → intracellular killing; specific granules (lactoferrin, lysozyme, CD11b) → extracellular release; gelatinase granules (metalloproteinases)
3. Innate Immunity — Mononuclear Phagocyte System
3.1 Monocyte-Macrophage Development
Embryonic macrophages: yolk sac week 3–4 (before first monocytes); large histiocytes from yolk sac progenitors, ส่วนใหญ่ MHC II negative
Fetal liver: monocytes = 70% hematopoietic cells ที่ 2nd month; ↓ to 1–2% ตอน erythroid predominates; first circulating monocytes ~5th month
Term cord blood: relative monocytosis; monocyte concentration ↑ linearly 22–42 weeks gestation (ข้อมูล >62,000 counts); ↑ further first 2 postnatal weeks
Monocyte → macrophage: ↑ diameter ×3+; ↑ cytoplasmic granules + vacuoles; ↑ surface receptors; ↑ phagocytic activity. Circulate 72h → migrate to tissue
3.2 Monocyte/Macrophage Function
Cord blood monocytes (term): adherence, random migration, bactericidal activity, superoxide, H₂O₂ production ~ adults. Exception: ↓ phagocytosis GBS (ดีขึ้นด้วย fibronectin + IgG opsonization)
Peripheral blood neonatal monocytes: chemotaxis impaired → ↓ influx ที่ inflammatory sites; chemotaxis ↓ progressively first week of life → slowly improves → reaches adult by age 6 years
Cytokine production defects: ↓ IFN-α, IL-8, IL-10, GM-CSF (vs adults); IL-1, IFN-γ, TNF comparable; ↓ fibronectin; ↓ leukotriene B4
M1 vs M2 macrophage polarization: M1 = pro-inflammatory; M2 = anti-inflammatory; macrophages shift M1 → M2 during resolution of inflammation
Alveolar macrophages: small pool in fetus → expands rapidly in early neonatal period (influx from circulation + in situ clonal expansion); appear to function normally in neonates examined
4. Humoral Innate Immunity
4.1 Complement System

Complement Pathways — Clinical Summary
Classic pathway: C1q binds IgG/IgM immune complexes → C4 → C2 → C3 convertase → C3b (opsonin) + C3a (anaphylatoxin); C5 → C5a (chemotaxis, anaphylatoxin) + C5b → MAC (C5b–C9). ต้องการ specific antibody → แทบไม่มีประโยชน์แรกเกิด. Alternative pathway: spontaneous C3b + surface of bacteria → C3bBb (properdin, B, D) → amplification loop → does NOT need antibody. Lectin pathway: MBL/ficolins bind carbohydrate patterns on pathogens → MASP-2 → C4 → C2 → C3 convertase — also antibody-independent. Neonatal levels (% adult): CH50: 52–81% (term), 32–78% (preterm); C3: 50–97% / 39–60%; C9: 14–55% / 33–56% — most components reach adult levels by 6–18 months. Clinical implication: alternative + lectin pathways คือ primary defense ใน neonates ก่อนมี specific antibody.
CR1 (C3b receptor): erythrocytes + phagocytes → immune complex clearance. CR3 (iC3b/Mac-1/CD11b): ถ้า genetic deficiency → severe recurrent bacterial infections
MBL: serum levels vary 1000-fold (polymorphisms ใน promoter region). Neonates มี ↓ MBL; ↓ levels in first week + ↓ preterm vs term; ↓ MBL ↔ ↑ risk neonatal sepsis
4.2 Fibronectin
Function: multifunctional glycoprotein; ↑ neutrophil + monocyte chemotaxis, adhesion, migration; ↑ phagocyte ingestion + killing of opsonized organisms
Neonatal levels: cord blood 120 µg/mL; term infants 220 µg/mL; preterm 30–31 wk: 152 µg/mL. ↓↓ ใน RDS, birth depression, sepsis, IUGR
4.3 CRP, Lactoferrin, Collectins

CRP: acute phase reactant (IL-6-driven, liver); opsonizes pneumococcal polysaccharide → complement activation → phagocytosis. Normal <1.6 mg/dL days 1–2; <1 mg/dL thereafter. Serial CRP useful ใน antimicrobial stewardship (normalizes in 5–6 days หลัง response to therapy)
IL-6: rises more rapidly than CRP → useful ใน early-onset sepsis workup. Elevated amniotic fluid IL-6 ↔ fetal inflammatory response + white matter injury + cerebral palsy ใน preterm

Lactoferrin: iron chelator; destabilizes microbial membranes; ↑ neutrophil function. ELFIN trial (Lancet 2019;393:423, n=2203): enteral bovine lactoferrin NO reduction ใน LOS risk ใน very preterm → ไม่แนะนำ routine use
SP-A + SP-D (surfactant collectins): type II AECs + club cells; ↑ third trimester; bind pathogens → ↑ phagocytosis. SP-D ↓ pulmonary inflammation; ↓ SP-D ↔ worse BPD outcome (Arroyo Respir Res 2021)
5. TLRs and Cytokine Network ใน Neonatal Sepsis
5.1 Toll-Like Receptors (TLRs)

TLR Biology — Pattern Recognition สำหรับ Neonatologist
TLRs = PRR (pathogen-associated molecular pattern receptors); transmembrane proteins; cytoplasmic domain homologous to IL-1 receptor → NF-κB activation. Key TLRs ใน NICU: TLR4 (LPS + gram-negative bacteria + RSV F protein) — needs CD14 + MD-2 coreceptors; TLR4 directly implicated ใน NEC + sepsis (Abreu & Arditi J Pediatr 2004); TLR2 (gram-positive: peptidoglycan, lipoteichoic acid, Staph epidermidis); TLR3 (dsRNA/viruses); TLR5 (flagellin/Salmonella); TLR7/8 (ssRNA/viral); TLR9 (bacterial CpG DNA). Neonatal defects: ↓ TLR4 + CD14 expression on APCs; ↓ MyD88; preterm < term in cord blood. Signaling: CD14 identifies + sorts LPS to TLR4 → TLR → NF-κB → cytokine cascade.
NLRP3 inflammasome: activated by DAMPs/ATP → caspase-1 → IL-1β + IL-18 maturation. Implicated ใน NEC, neonatal brain injury, BPD (Omer et al Neonatology 2020)
Pentoxifylline: downregulates TLR4 signaling → anti-inflammatory ใน monocytes; synergy กับ dexamethasone + azithromycin. Currently under study ใน neonatal sepsis
5.2 Cytokine Cascade ใน Sepsis
Sequence: low-dose endotoxin challenge → TNF peaks 90 min → pro-inflammatory cytokines follow → anti-inflammatory (IL-10) peaks hours later
TNF (term neonates): ↓ production vs adults; cord blood preterm << term << adults; postnatal samples normal — ยืนยันว่า cord blood ≠ neonatal immune function
IL-6: monocytes term → adequate after LPS stimulation; preterm → ↓ regardless of stimulus; clinical use: ↑ rises faster than CRP → early sepsis biomarker
Compensatory Anti-Inflammatory Response Syndrome (CARS): IL-1ra + soluble receptors ↑ → monocyte deactivation/immunoparalysis → ↑ infection risk → ↑ mortality + multiorgan dysfunction
PAF (Platelet Activating Factor): potent phospholipid mediator; ↑ ใน NEC; causes hypotension, capillary leak, pulmonary HTN, neutropenia, thrombocytopenia, intestinal ischemic necrosis ใน animals
Chemokines: ~50 ligands, 20 receptors; CXC → neutrophils + T cells; CC → monocytes + T cells. Chemotactic factor generation deficient ใน neonates
6. Acquired Immunity — T Lymphocytes
6.1 T Cell Development
Thymus: pharyngeal pouches 3–4 → week 6–7; cortex + medulla differentiate week 10; Hassall corpuscles week 12
Week 7: lymphoid progenitors enter thymus (CD7+CD45+, double negative CD4-CD8-) → double positive (CD4+CD8+) week 10 → single positive (CD4+ or CD8+) late gestation
Week 12: occasional CD3+ cells ใน peripheral blood; >50% T cells at 22 weeks; CD3+ cells ใน liver + spleen at 13 weeks

X-SCID mechanism: mutation ใน γc chain (common γ subunit) ของ IL-2 receptor → ยัง affects IL-4, IL-7, IL-9, IL-13, IL-15 receptors → T-cell aplasia + defective B-cell Ig production (no T-cell help); X-linked inheritance
6.2 T Cell Activation + Signal Requirements
Signal 1: TCR + MHC/peptide → LCK → ZAP70 → LAT → transcription factors
Signal 2 (costimulation): CD28 (T cell) + B7-1/CD80 or B7-2/CD86 (APC) → IL-2 production + prevent anergy/apoptosis
Signal 3 (cytokines): needed for CD4+ subset differentiation: IL-12 → Th1 (STAT4/T-bet); IL-4 → Th2 (STAT6/GATA-3); TGF-β+IL-6 → Th17 (RORγt/STAT3); TGF-β+STAT5 → Treg (Foxp3)
6.3 Neonatal T Cell Defects — Th2 Skewing

Th2 Skewing ใน Neonatal CD4+ T Cells — Epigenetic Basis
Mechanism: Hypomethylated CNS1 (conserved noncoding sequence 1) at Th2 cytokine locus ใน neonatal CD4+ cells → immediate release of IL-4 + IL-13 on antigenic stimulation → Th2 effectors. Compounding factor: IL-4 จาก Th2 cells acts on IL-4R/IL-13R heteroreceptor บน Th1 cells → Th1 cell apoptosis. ↓ DC-derived IL-12 → further ↓ Th1 differentiation. Result: ↓ cell-mediated immunity → ↑ vulnerability ต่อ intracellular pathogens. Plasticity preserved: BCG vaccination → adult Th1 response. Altering antigen dose or adding costimulatory molecules → overcomes Th2 skewing in mice.

CD4+ counts: cord blood total T-cell count higher vs adults; but both CD4+ + CD8+ ↓ with ↓ GA. Treg cells ↑ ใน preterm > term > adult cord blood → immune suppression → ↓ Treg/CD4 ratio ใน NEC ileum (protective role of Tregs)
CD8+ (cytotoxic T cells): relatively normal; functional memory CD8+ against congenital CMV; CTL cytotoxicity at term <20% of adult (perforin expression ~30%)
CXCL8 (IL-8): major T cell effector function ใน human newborns → activates antimicrobial neutrophils; provoked by antigen receptor engagement of T cells (Gibbons Nat Med 2014)
6.4 Dendritic Cells

Neonatal DC defects: ↓ costimulatory molecules (CD40, CD80, CD86); ↓ MHC class II; ↓ IL-12p70 production; ↓ IFN-α in pDCs. Outcome: ↓ naive T cell priming → tolerance + susceptibility to viral/intracellular pathogens
pDC:mDC ratio: cord blood 3:1 (vs adult 1:3); pDCs respond poorly to TLR7/TLR9 agonists. Flt3L → ↑ pDC differentiation + IFN production → potential immunotherapy
Neonatal DCs programmed to suppress inflammation at baseline → protects from alloreaction with maternal antigens → but ↑ susceptibility to viral pathogens
7. Acquired Immunity — B Lymphocytes + Immunoglobulins
7.1 B Cell Development
Pre-B cell: fetal liver week 7–8; cytoplasmic µ chain (IgM heavy chain); diversity generated at this stage by V(D)J recombination (chr 14: VH/DH/JH; chr 2: κ; chr 22: λ)
Immature B cell: week 8–9; surface IgM + complement receptors; if exposed to antigen without T cell help → clonal anergy (= tolerance to self-antigens)
Mature B cell: surface IgM + IgD; week 15 = adult-level circulating B cells. B cells highest ใน spleen (30%), blood (35%), lymph nodes (13%)
Isotype switching: B cells switching เริ่ม week 12 (IgG/IgA); driven by CD40-CD40L interaction + IL-4 (IgE) + IL-5 (IgA)
7.2 Neonatal B Cell Defects
Cell-intrinsic: ↓ CD40, CD80, CD86 → defective T-cell interaction; ↓ CD21 (marginal zone B cells); ↓ TACI expression (especially preterm) → impaired B-cell activation

Environmental: maternal antibodies can inhibit neonatal B cells from recognizing vaccine antigens; ↓ C3 → ↓ antigen-antibody complex formation; ↓ follicular DC maturation → ↓ somatic hypermutation + class switching

CD5+ B cells (B-1 cells): ~90% of cord blood B cells are CD5+ B-1 cells (adult = 25–35%); spontaneously secrete IgM → first-line defense against encapsulated bacteria (S. pneumoniae); ↓ to adult levels by adolescence

Vaccine response: neonatal B cells may preferentially generate memory B cells > plasma cells due to lower BCR affinity → delayed high-titer antibody responses → justify vaccine schedules at 2/4/6 months

Under-2-year inability to respond to polysaccharide antigens (pneumococcal, Hib): T-cell-independent antigens → B-1 cells can respond but B-2 class switching requires T help → use conjugated vaccines ใน <2 years
7.3 Immunoglobulin Classes
7.4 Antibody Production ใน Fetus/Neonate

IgM synthesis: fetal cells ใน vitro ≥8 weeks. IgG slightly later. IgA: ~30 weeks gestation. แต่ in vivo: fetus makes little antibody ก่อนเกิด (sterile env; T-cell suppression of B-cell differentiation)

At birth: most IgG = maternally transferred (IgM <10% adult); IgG nadir ที่ 3–4 เดือน = physiologic hypogammaglobulinemia (maternal IgG catabolized, infant production lags). Adult IgG levels: 4–6 years. Adult IgA: puberty

Congenital infection markers: ↑ cord blood IgM → fetal infection (syphilis, rubella, CMV, Toxoplasma); specific IgM reliable evidence ของ intrauterine infection (IgM ≠ cross placenta). ↑ IgM + IgA also ใน maternal-fetal transplacental bleed
8. NK Cells และ Innate Lymphoid Cells (ILCs)
8.1 Natural Killer Cells
Phenotype: CD56 + CD16 (FcRIIIa); also CD2, LFA-1, IL-2Rβ, IL-12R, IL-15R. Large granular lymphocytes. 10–15% peripheral blood lymphocytes
Detectible ≥6 weeks gestation; ↑ until birth; cord blood 10–15% lymphocytes = comparable to adult %, but phenotype different
Fetal NK cells: 50–80% express CD3 (unusual vs adult where very few do); only 30–50% express CD16 (vs >90% adult); ↓ CD56 + CD57 vs adult
Cytolytic activity: ↑ with GA; at term: only 50–80% of adult cytolytic activity; ADCC ↓ ใน fetal NK cells
Killing mechanism: perforin/granzyme pathway + Fas/FasL apoptosis pathway; recognition = absent/↓ MHC class I on target cell
8.2 Innate Lymphoid Cells (ILCs)
3 groups: ILC1 (T-bet, IFN-γ → intracellular pathogens); ILC2 (GATA-3, IL-4/IL-5/IL-13 → allergy/tissue repair); ILC3 (RORγt, IL-17A/IL-22 → mucosal defense + barrier)
ILC2 cells present ใน cord blood; may ↑ ใน male neonates vs female. ILC3 identified as source of ↑ IL-17 ใน preeclampsia + gestational diabetes
Unlike T/B cells: no antigen specificity; function as part of innate immune system; mirror Th1/Th2/Th17 cytokine profiles without MHC restriction
9. Passive Immunity — Placental Transfer + Breast Milk
9.1 Placental IgG Transport

IgG Placental Transfer — Critical Timing
Mechanism: FcRn (neonatal Fc receptor) บน syncytiotrophoblast → active transport ของ IgG เท่านั้น. Timeline: fetal IgG = 5–10% maternal levels ที่ 17–22 wk → accelerated transfer เริ่ม 28–30 wk → neonatal IgG exceeds maternal levels by 20–30% at term (IgG = 600–1670 mg/dL at birth). Implications: Preterm infants ↓ IgG proportional to degree of prematurity; SGA infants also ↓ IgG (placental dysfunction). IgG nadir 3–4 months (physiologic hypogammaglobulinemia) → ↑ infection risk ใน ex-preterm ที่ discharged. Transfer ↓ progressively after 44 weeks (placental aging). นำไปสู่: vaccine ที่ transferred well = viral vaccines + diphtheria + tetanus toxoid; vaccine ที่ไม่ transferred = anti-gram-negative IgM.
9.2 Breast Milk Immunology

sIgA: predominant Ig ใน breast milk; dimer + J chain + secretory component → resistant to protease + gastric acid; mucosal immunity GI + respiratory tract. Homing mechanism: B cells sensitized ใน maternal Peyer patches → migrate to mammary glands → plasma cells → secrete antibodies specific ต่อ maternal enteric flora → protects infant's GI tract colonization
IgG constant during lactation; IgA + IgM highest ใน colostrum → ↓ first 5 days → stable 175 days. Breast milk IgA specific ต่อ enteric bacteria + viruses (polio, echo, coxsackie)
Breast milk macrophages: 80% of milk leukocytes; phagocytic; contain IgA; synthesize lysozyme (300× human > cow milk; stable in acid), lactoferrin, C3/C4 complement; capable of killing gram+ + gram-

T lymphocytes: 50% of milk lymphocytes early lactation; milk T cells = different specificity vs maternal peripheral blood (respond to E. coli K1 antigen; unresponsive to C. albicans; suggests GI homing)
SP-A, SP-D, MBL: collectins ใน breast milk → ↑ phagocytosis ของ GBS + other pathogens. MBL activates classic complement
Nonimmune factors: epidermal growth factor, prostaglandins, thyroid hormones, prolactin, erythropoietin, somatostatin, lysozyme

Clinical implication: only breastmilk has shown reduction in sepsis incidence ใน neonatal sepsis prevention trials (Wynn et al J Perinatol 2009); none of other interventions (IVIG, G-CSF, GM-CSF, lactoferrin, probiotics) improved mortality
10. Algorithm — Immune Evaluation + Immunomodulation

rows: CBC + differential; consider sepsis/infection vs maternal preeclampsia vs IVH vs hemolytic disease. Schmutz ranges สำหรับ GA-specific ANC
I/T ratio; immature myelocytes → poor prognosis. Blood culture + empiric antibiotics. G-CSF ถ้า severe depletion (individualized)
Consider LAD type I (CD18 deficiency): CBC often shows neutrophilia (can't migrate out); CD18 flow cytometry; consult immunology
Cord blood IgM >20 mg/dL (specific IgM to agent); IgA ↑ → intrauterine infection or maternal-fetal bleed
Ex-preterm at 3–4 months: ↓ IgG (maternal catabolized + infant production lags) → screen IgG level ถ้า recurrent infections; IVIG ถ้า <200 mg/dL + infections
INIS trial (NEJM 2011): no benefit. Don't use routine IVIG for sepsis treatment
Not recommended routine (PROGRAMS study). Individualized: severe neutropenia + sepsis may benefit, not prophylaxis
Follow chronologic age (not corrected age) for DTP/Hib/PCV13/Rotavirus schedule; influenza vaccine ≥6 months actual age; RSV prophylaxis (Palivizumab) for eligible preterm

blocks.text: 11. NICU Practical — Immune Function ที่ Bedside
ใช้ Schmutz ANC reference ranges ไม่ใช่ term-infant ranges สำหรับ preterm — เลี่ยง over-diagnosis/under-diagnosis neutropenia
Complement deficiency → severity ↑ ใน preterm: ถ้า encapsulated organism (GBS, Strep pneumo, Hib) → need opsonization → preterm มีทั้ง ↓ complement + ↓ specific IgG → ↑ risk overwhelming sepsis
TLR4 + NEC: formula feeding → ↑ TLR4 activation ใน immature gut → NEC; breast milk + lactoferrin + sIgA → ↓ TLR4 overactivation → protective mechanism ของ breast milk
CD64 เป็น early sepsis biomarker: ↑ ใน both preterm + term neonates; not affected by respiratory distress/PROM → useful adjunct ใน equivocal presentations
IFN-γ + white matter damage: ↑ postnatal IFN-γ ใน preterm ↔ white matter injury + cerebral palsy → chorioamnionitis + fetal inflammatory response → consider neuro surveillance ใน FIRS infants
Breast milk → เป็นมากกว่าแค่ nutrition: sIgA + lactoferrin + macrophages + T/B cells + collectins = living immune supplement — document donor milk vs own mother milk ใน NEC prevention protocol
Preterm infants have delayed postnatal maturation ของ neutrophil function: chemotaxis, phagocytosis, NET formation ล้วน impaired → skin/line care สำคัญมาก (bundle ป้องกัน CLABSI/CAUTI)
12. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Preterm NSP เล็กมาก: Sepsis = Depletion + Neutropenia = Bad Sign
Neutrophil storage pool (NSP) ของ preterm = เล็กมาก (~10th adult/kg); proliferating pool (NPP) ก็ตาม. Sepsis ใน preterm → rapid NSP depletion → neutropenia → left shift (metamyelocytes/myelocytes ↑) → ↑ mortality (NEC surgery). Schmutz ranges: <28 wk ต่างจาก term มาก (ANC 1300–15,300 ที่ 72–240h vs term 2700–13,000). ใช้ reference range ให้ถูก GA ก่อน label neutropenia. CD64 ↑ → early infection marker แม้ ANC ยังปกติ.

PEARL 2 — Complement ≠ Usable แรกเกิด: Alternative + Lectin Pathway คือ First Line
Classic complement pathway ต้องการ specific antibody → IgG จาก placenta ไม่ครอบคลุม gram-negative bacteria (maternal IgM ไม่ข้ามรก) → classic pathway essentially useless ใน first encounter กับ gram-negative bacteria. Preterm มี CH50 เพียง 32–78% และ C9 เพียง 33–56% adult. Alternative + lectin pathways คือ defense สำคัญ. MBL polymorphism + ↓ levels ↔ ↑ neonatal sepsis risk. ดังนั้น lectin + alternative pathway deficiency ใน preterm = clinically relevant risk.

PEARL 3 — TLR4 → NEC; NLRP3 → BPD + Brain Injury: Inflammation เชื่อมทุก Complication
TLR4 overactivation ใน immature gut epithelium → NEC; formula > breast milk ใน triggering TLR4. NLRP3 inflammasome → caspase-1 → IL-1β + IL-18 maturation → tissue damage → NEC, BPD, IVH, NE. Pentoxifylline downregulates TLR4 → anti-inflammatory synergy (under study). IL-6 + IFN-γ ↑ ใน fetal inflammatory response → white matter injury + cerebral palsy. สรุป: systemic inflammation in preterm = multiorgan disease — treat sepsis aggressively ลด secondary organ damage.

PEARL 4 — Physiologic Hypogammaglobulinemia: Nadir 3–4 เดือน ใน Ex-Preterm
Maternal IgG (t½ = 21d) ถูก catabolize → infant's own B-cell production lag (นานกว่าใน preterm) → nadir IgG at 3–4 months (worse in ex-preterm: nadir lower + longer). Normal full-term nadir: ~300 mg/dL. Ex-preterm อาจ <200 mg/dL → pathologic. Suspect ถ้า recurrent/severe bacterial infections in ex-28wk infant at 4 months. Measure IgG level; IVIG if <200 mg/dL + infections. Vaccine schedule: use chronologic age (not corrected) for standard vaccines.

PEARL 5 — Only Breast Milk Reduces Sepsis: เป็นยาชีวภาพ ไม่ใช่แค่อาหาร
Of all immunomodulatory agents studied in neonatal sepsis (IVIG, G-CSF, GM-CSF, lactoferrin [ELFIN 2019 negative], probiotics, anti-staphylococcal antibodies, pentoxifylline) — only breast milk has shown reduction in sepsis incidence. Mechanism: sIgA (homing from maternal Peyer patches → gut-specific antibodies), lactoferrin (iron chelation + membrane disruption), macrophages (lysozyme 300× cow milk), collectins (SP-A/SP-D/MBL → opsonization of GBS), live lymphocytes. Own mother's milk > pasteurized donor milk for immune properties. Every drop matters — express every 3h.
13. Evidence Update — Key Studies
Olin A et al Cell 2018;174:1277: stereotypic immune system development in newborn children; postnatal samples more reflective of neonatal immune function vs cord blood
Yost CC et al Blood 2009;113:6419: NET formation: novel innate immune deficiency ใน human neonates — neonatal PMNs markedly deficient in NET formation
Vincent D et al Sci Rep 2018;8:12612: NEC is likely NETs-dependent; markers of NETosis predictive of NEC in mice + humans
Schmutz N et al J Perinatol 2008;28:275: revised ANC reference ranges 23–42 weeks (n=30,354 CBCs); replaced Manroe + Mouzinho charts
INIS Collaborative Group (Brocklehurst) NEJM 2011;365:1201: IVIG for neonatal sepsis: NO improvement ใน mortality หรือ neurodevelopmental outcome at 2 years
ELFIN Trial Investigators Lancet 2019;393:423: bovine lactoferrin (n=2203 very preterm): NO reduction ใน risk of LOS — not recommended routine
Marlow N et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2015;100:F320: PROGRAMS study 5-year outcomes: GM-CSF prophylaxis → ↑ neutrophils + suggestion worse respiratory outcomes → not recommended
McGreal EP et al Immunobiology 2012;217:176: off to a slow start — complement underdevelopment in term newborns; more substantial in preterm
Schlapbach LJ et al Clin Infect Dis 2010;51:153: lectin pathway differential role ใน neonatal sepsis susceptibility; ↓ MBL + ficolins ใน culture-proven sepsis
Gibbons D et al Nat Med 2014;20:1206: CXCL8 (IL-8) production = signatory T cell effector function of human newborns (TLR-costimulated)
Correa-Rocha R et al Pediatr Res 2012;71:590: preterm neonates: leukopenia + lymphopenia; ↑ Treg values + ↓ IL-7
Omer M et al Neonatology 2020;117:545: NLRP3 inflammasome emerging role ใน NEC, neonatal brain injury, BPD
Arroyo R, Kingma PS Respir Res 2021;22:141: SP-D and BPD: ↓ SP-D ↔ worse BPD; rhSP-D potential preventive agent
Hibbert J et al PLoS One 2022;17:e0264768: composition of early-life leukocyte populations in preterm with/without LOS; sepsis-induced immune suppression in serial samples
Jung E et al Semin Fetal Neonatal Med 2020;25:101146: fetal inflammatory response syndrome (FIRS): origins, pathophysiology, diagnosis, obstetric implications
Dammann O, Leviton A Pediatr Res 2014;75:376: intermittent or sustained systemic inflammation ↔ preterm brain injury; duration matters — ↑ duration = ↑ adverse neurodevelopmental effect
14. Self-Check Q&A
Q: Preterm neutrophil storage pool (NSP) เล็กกว่า adult เท่าไหร่ และมีนัยทางคลินิกอะไร?

ตอบ: NSP ของ preterm มีขนาดเล็กมาก (แทบไม่มี mature reserve); NPP ก็ ~10th ของ adult (per kg BW). Clinical implication: sepsis → rapid depletion ของ NSP → profound neutropenia + left shift (metamyelocytes/myelocytes ↑) → ↑ mortality (โดยเฉพาะ surgical NEC). ดังนั้น neutropenia ใน septic preterm ≠ benign — มัน represent immune exhaustion. ใช้ Schmutz GA-specific ranges สำหรับ ANC interpretation.
Q: Neonatal PMN chemotaxis: มี defect ใน term และ preterm ต่างกันอย่างไร? เมื่อไหรจะ normal?

ตอบ: Term neonates: impaired at birth → normal by 2 weeks after birth. Preterm 34–36 wk: normal by 40–42 weeks PCA. Preterm <34 wk: remains impaired compared to adults even at 42 wk PCA. Mechanism: ↓ calcium mobilization; aberrant NF-κB; ↓ Mac-1 → impaired extracellular matrix interaction. Gram-negative sepsis → ↓ chemotaxis further; superficial infection → ↑.
Q: Complement: classic vs alternative vs lectin pathway — อันไหนสำคัญกว่าใน neonate?

ตอบ: Classic pathway ต้องการ specific antibody (IgG/IgM) → แทบไม่มีประโยชน์แรกเกิด (maternal IgM ไม่ข้ามรก; ไม่มี specific IgG ต่อ gram-negative bacteria). Alternative + lectin pathways = primary neonatal defense: alternative → spontaneous C3b activation on bacterial surfaces; lectin → MBL + ficolins binds carbohydrate on pathogens. ↓ MBL ↔ ↑ neonatal sepsis. ↓ lectin pathway activity ↔ culture-proven sepsis (Schlapbach 2010).
Q: X-SCID: กลไกคืออะไรและทำไมถึงส่งผลกระทบต่อหลาย cytokine receptors?

ตอบ: X-SCID = mutation ใน IL-2 receptor γ chain (γc / common γ subunit; gene X-linked). γc เป็น shared subunit ของ receptors สำหรับ IL-2, IL-4, IL-7, IL-9, IL-13, IL-15 → mutation ทำให้ signaling ของ cytokines เหล่านี้ทั้งหมดล้มเหลว → T cell aplasia + B cell dysfunction (no T-cell help → predominantly IgM secretion). Phenotype: T-cell absent + B cells present (แต่ dysfunctional). SCID screening ด้วย TREC (T-cell receptor excision circles) ใน newborn screen.
Q: Th2 skewing ใน neonatal T cells: กลไก epigenetic คืออะไร + ผลทางคลินิก?

ตอบ: Hypomethylated CNS1 (conserved noncoding sequence 1) ที่ Th2 cytokine locus ใน neonatal CD4+ cells → ทันที antigenic stimulation → IL-4 + IL-13 released → Th2 effectors. Compounding: IL-4 จาก Th2 cells → IL-4R/IL-13R heteroreceptor บน Th1 cells → Th1 apoptosis. ↓ DC-derived IL-12 ยิ่งกระทบ Th1. Clinical: ↓ cell-mediated immunity → ↑ susceptibility ต่อ intracellular pathogens (Listeria, CMV, TB). อย่างไรก็ตาม BCG → adult-type Th1 response → vaccines work.
Q: CD16 vs CD32 deficiency ใน preterm neutrophils: ต่างกันอย่างไร?

ตอบ: CD16 (FcRIII): ↓ ใน preterm; normalizes within 3 weeks of life. CD32 (FcRII): ↓ ใน preterm; does NOT correct with time. Clinical significance: CD32 = high-affinity receptor for IgG2 (critical against encapsulated bacteria: S. pneumo, Hib, GBS) → persistent CD32 deficiency = ongoing vulnerability ต่อ encapsulated bacteria throughout NICU stay. CD64 (FcRI): ↑ ใน neonates vs adults → early sepsis biomarker, not affected by RDS/PROM.
Q: Physiologic hypogammaglobulinemia: เกิดอย่างไร + nadir อยู่เมื่อใด + significance ใน preterm?

ตอบ: Mechanism: maternal IgG (t½ 21d) catabolized → infant B cell production lags → nadir ที่ 3–4 เดือน. ปกติ: full-term nadir ~300 mg/dL. Preterm: nadir ต่ำกว่า + ช้ากว่า (อาจถึง 6 months, nadir <200 mg/dL). IgG level 600–1670 mg/dL at birth (term) แต่ลดลงอย่างรวดเร็ว. เมื่อ ex-preterm 28-wk มีอายุ 4 months → recurrent bacterial infections → ตรวจ IgG level; IVIG ถ้า <200 mg/dL + infections. Vaccine schedule ใช้ chronologic age ไม่ใช่ corrected age.
Q: Breast milk sIgA สร้างอย่างไรและทำไมจึง specific ต่อ neonatal gut?

ตอบ: Homing mechanism: B cells sensitized ใน maternal GI Peyer patches → migrate via lymphatics → home ไปที่ mammary glands → differentiate into plasma cells → secrete IgA antibodies specific ต่อ maternal enteric bacteria + viruses. ดังนั้น breast milk antibody composition ≠ maternal serum — anti-enteric titers much higher ใน colostrum vs serum. Clinical: infant colonized primarily with maternal flora → maternal gut-specific sIgA protects infant's gut. Secretory component → resistant to proteases + gastric acid → active ใน entire GI tract. No evidence sIgA enters systemic circulation ใน human neonate.
Q: TLR4 มีบทบาทอะไรใน NEC? และ TLR signaling ใน neonates แตกต่างจาก adults อย่างไร?

ตอบ: TLR4 = pattern recognition receptor สำหรับ LPS (gram-negative bacteria) + RSV F protein; needs CD14 + MD-2 coreceptors. TLR4 overactivation ใน immature gut epithelium → NEC. Neonatal defects ใน TLR signaling: ↓ TLR4 + CD14 expression on APCs; ↓ MyD88 (key adapter molecule); ↓ upregulation of TLR4/CD14 after LPS stimulation → damped inflammatory response at baseline (protective ต่อ alloreaction) แต่ ↑ susceptibility ต่อ infection. Formula feeding → ↑ TLR4 activation → NEC; breast milk → ↓ TLR4 overactivation (sIgA/lactoferrin).
Q: IVIG + G-CSF/GM-CSF ใน neonatal sepsis: evidence บอกอะไร?

ตอบ: IVIG: INIS trial (NEJM 2011, n=2416): NO improvement ใน mortality หรือ neurodevelopment at 2 years. Likely reason: current IVIG formulations ↓ antibodies ต่อ neonatal pathogens. ไม่แนะนำ routine. G-CSF/GM-CSF: ↑ neutrophil count + improve function qualitatively; แต่ not recommended routine (PROGRAMS study: GM-CSF prophylaxis → no sepsis reduction + possible ↑ respiratory morbidity at 2 + 5 years). Individualized consideration ใน severe symptomatic neutropenia + sepsis — ไม่ใช่ prophylaxis.

Day 147 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.50
บทต่อไป: Chapter 50 — Postnatal Bacterial Infections. เตรียมตัว: epidemiology ของ early-onset vs late-onset sepsis ใน NICU; organisms ที่พบบ่อย (GBS, E. coli, CoNS, Staph aureus, gram-negative rods); diagnosis: blood culture, CBC, CRP, IL-6, PCT, PCR panels; empiric antibiotic regimens (GBS/gram-neg vs CONS/nosocomial); antimicrobial stewardship + de-escalation; CLABSI prevention bundle; antifungal prophylaxis threshold; risk-based approaches (EOS calculator).
